{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Ketawakamiya Shrine
気多若宮神社 | image = | width = 250 | caption = | type = | dedication = [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami | founded = | closed = | founder = | priest = Hito Higashimichi | address = 1297 Kami-keta, [[Hida, Gifu|Hida]]
[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] | phone = | website = }} {{nihongo|'''Ketawakamiya Shrine'''|気多若宮神社|Keta Wakamiya Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in the city of [[Hida, Gifu|Hida]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Gifu Prefecture]]
{{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:気多若宮神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Wakamiya Shrine
気多若宮神社 | image = Keta wakamiya.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Keta Wakamiya Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|14|22.17|N|137|11|52.38|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami | founded_by = | established = [[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 1297 Kami-keta, Hida-shi, Gifu-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = Hito Higashimichi }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Wakamiya Shrine'''|気多若宮神社|Keta Wakamiya Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in the city of [[Hida, Gifu|Hida]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main [[Hadaka Matsuri|naked festivals]] in Japan and is designated as an [[Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Wakamiya Shrine

It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main [[Hadaka Matsuri|naked festivals]] in Japan and is designated as an [[Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Shrine]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushigenzaisya]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Wakamiya Shrine
気多若宮神社 | image = Keta wakamiya.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Keta Wakamiya Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|14|22.17|N|137|11|52.38|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami | founded_by = | established = [[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 1297 Kami-keta, Hida-shi, Gifu-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = Hito Higashimichi }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Wakamiya Shrine'''|気多若宮神社|Keta Wakamiya Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in the city of [[Hida, Gifu|Hida]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this [[shrine]] is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main [[Hadaka Matsuri|naked festivals]] in Japan and is designated as an [[Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Taisha]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia33]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}}

It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this [[shrine]] is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. {{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Wakamiya Shrine
気多若宮神社 | image = Keta wakamiya.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Keta Wakamiya Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|14|22.17|N|137|11|52.38|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami | founded_by = | established = [[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 1297 Kami-keta, Hida-shi, Gifu-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = Hito Higashimichi }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Wakamiya Shrine'''|気多若宮神社|Keta Wakamiya Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in the city of [[Hida, Gifu|Hida]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this [[shrine]] is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main [[Hadaka Matsuri|naked festivals]] in Japan and is designated as an [[Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Taisha]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] [[sn:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]]

It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this [[shrine]] is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main [[Hadaka Matsuri|naked festivals]] in Japan and is designated as an [[Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Taisha]] *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] [[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

気多若宮神社 | image = Keta wakamiya.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Keta Wakamiya Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|14|22.17|N|137|11|52.38|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami | founded_by = | established = [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 1297 Kami-keta, Hida-shi, Gifu-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = Hito Higashimichi }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Wakamiya Shrine'''|気多若宮神社|Keta Wakamiya Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this [[shrine]] is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main [[:en:Hadaka Matsuri|naked festivals]] in Japan and is designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Taisha]] *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] }} [[sn:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] [[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Keta Wakamiya]] {{Commons category|Keta Wakamiya-jinja}} [[fr:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]] [[ja:気多若宮神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

[[ca:Keta Wakamiya]][[co:commons:Category:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]][[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]][[fr:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]][[ja:気多若宮神社]] [[ca:Keta Wakamiya]][[co:commons:Category:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]][[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]][[fr:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]][[ja:気多若宮神社]] [[ca:Keta Wakamiya]][[sn:commons:Category:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]][[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]][[fr:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]][[ja:気多若宮神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Keta Wakamiya Shrine
気多若宮神社 | image = File:Keta wakamiya.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Keta Wakamiya Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.23949167|N|137.19788333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} | date_destroyed = | location = 1297 Kami-keta, Hida-shi, Gifu-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = Hito Higashimichi | location = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} }} {{nihongo|'''Keta Wakamiya Shrine'''|気多若宮神社|Keta Wakamiya Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is commonly referred to as "Sugimoto-sama" (杉本さま). ==History== The original construction of this [[shrine]] is unknown, but it is said to have been constructed during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. [[Ōkuninushi]] and Kinomata-no-kami (木俣神) are the main gods of the shrine, but [[Amaterasu]] is also worshipped here. ==Recognition== Keta Wakamiya Shrine is one of the shrines included in the Furukawa festival, which is one of the three main {{ill|Hadaka Matsuri|en|Hadaka Matsuri|WD=Q2616539|lt=naked festivals}} in Japan and is designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Keta Taisha]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] [[ca:Keta Wakamiya]] {{Commons category|Keta Wakamiya-jinja}} [[fr:Keta Wakamiya-jinja]] [[ja:気多若宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3195586}} [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
